content.problemStatement:
Alice is given a binary string SSS of length NNN.

She can perform the following operations on string SSS:


Select an index 1≤i≤∣S∣1\le i\le |S|1≤i≤∣S∣ and delete the character SiS_iSi​.;
Select an index 1≤i≤∣S∣1\le i\le |S|1≤i≤∣S∣ and flip the character SiS_iSi​. Note that while flipping 111 becomes 000 and vice-versa.
Find the **lexicographically smallest** string Alice can obtain using **at most** KKK operations.

Note that string XXX is said to be lexicographically smaller than string YYY if:


String XXX is a prefix of string YYY, or;
The smallest index iii where XXX and YYY differ, Xi<YiX_i \lt Y_iXi​<Yi​.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — denoting length of the string and the maximum number of operations, respectively.
The next line contains a binary string SSS of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **lexicographically smallest** string that Alice can make.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Alice will select index 111 and remove S1S_1S1​. The resulting string is 000000.
Test case 222: Alice cannot perform any operation. The resulting string is 111111.
